**About the role** 




 In this role, you sit at the intersection of customer, product, and engineering. As a technical partner in
 



 our sales processes, you'll be responsible for the technical aspects of customer interactions, making
 



 sure we design and deliver solutions that truly make a difference. You will also ensure that solution
 



 architect deliverables are high quality and well defined, while continuously improving our solution
 



 architecture processes and ways of working. Working closely with our delivery, product, and
 



 engineering teams, you'll make sure that what we propose is what we build, and over time you will
 



 become a trusted advisor that customers turn to whenever they're scaling or rethinking their setup.
 



  





**Who are you?** 




 We are looking for someone who combines deep technical competence with strong communication
 



 skills. You thrive in customer facing roles, have a solution-oriented mindset, and are motivated by
 



 building long term business relationships. You likely bring proven experience in solution architecture
 



 along with extensive knowledge of the cloud native industry, including a few years of working with
 



 public clouds and cloud native technologies. Familiarity with the Elastisys technology stack is a plus,
 



 as is the ability to explain complex solutions clearly and engagingly.
 



  





 An academic degree in cloud computing or similar is one way to have built this. It's your competence
 



 and curiosity that count. If you recognize yourself in most of this but not all, apply anyway. We'd rather
 



 hear from you and talk it through.
 



  





**Why Elastisys?** 




 We're a team of 40+ engineers, researchers, and innovators working on cloud native problems that
 



 genuinely matter: secure, compliant infrastructure for organizations where getting it right isn't optional.
 



  





 Security, knowledge sharing, and care for each other aren't values on a wall, they're how we make
 



 decisions every day.
 



  





 Here's what that looks like in practice:
 


* A hybrid workplace with a real blend of office and remote work, so the role fits into the rest of your life
* A paid wellness hour every week, plus a yearly wellness grant
* Paid individual training each year, so you stay at the edge of your field
* Occupational pension and the expected basics, handled properly
* Time and space for open source contribution as part of the work, not on top of it
* A team that shares what it knows, from pair work to internal tech talks to mentoring that's structured, not accidental



  





 If you want to grow technically, lead when it matters, and work somewhere different perspectives are
 



 treated as an asset rather than a talking point, we'd love to hear from you.
 



  





 Curious but not sure you tick every box? Reach out to Hanna Grahn and let's talk it through. We'd rather have the conversation than miss the right person.

title: Avfallstekniker – Huddinge – Heltid
description:
**Plats** 
 : Huddinge
   

  

**Omfattning** 
 : Heltid
   

  

**Arbetstider** 
 : 6-15, måndag till fredag
   

  

**Start** 
 : Så fort som möjligt
   

  

 Vill du arbeta praktiskt, bidra till en bättre miljö och vara en del av ett engagerat team? Nu söker vi en
 **avfallstekniker** 
 till en väletablerad och modern avfallsanläggning. Här får du en viktig roll i arbetet med sortering och behandling av material – med fokus på kvalitet, säkerhet och hållbarhet.
   

  

 Rekryteringen och anställningen hanteras av
 **Simplex Bemanning** 
 . Du blir anställd av Simplex och uthyrd till vår kund.
   

  

 Om tjänsten
   

  

 Som avfallstekniker arbetar du med löpande sortering och behandling av material som inkommer från företagskunder och återvinningscentraler. Du säkerställer att avfallet hanteras korrekt för materialåtervinning, energiutvinning eller deponering.
   

  

 Arbetet utförs med hjälp av maskiner såsom:
   

  

* Hjullastare
* Materialhanterare
* Truck


 Du är en viktig del i att skapa en säker, effektiv och välfungerande anläggning – både för kollegor och besökare.
   

  

 Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Sortera och hantera inkommande material enligt gällande rutiner
* Säkerställa ordning och en trygg arbetsmiljö på anläggningen
* Utföra dagliga kontroller och förebyggande underhåll av maskiner
* Rapportera avvikelser samt behov av service och reparation
* Kvalitetssäkra material och uppmärksamma felaktig sortering
* Bidra med förbättrings- och utvecklingsförslag inom verksamheten


 Vi söker dig som har
   

  

**Krav**
* Hjulastarutbildning för godshantering
* Truckkort A2–A4 och C2
* B-körkort
* Gymnasieutbildning eller motsvarande
* Grundläggande IT-kunskaper
* Goda kunskaper i svenska (tal och skrift)


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av att köra hjulgrävare/materialhanterare
* Yrkesbevis
* Kunskaper i engelska


 Personliga egenskaper
   

  

 För att trivas i rollen ser vi att du:
   

  

* Kan arbeta i högt tempo och hantera pressade situationer
* Är flexibel och anpassningsbar
* Har mycket god samarbetsförmåga
* Har god självinsikt och kan ge och ta emot konstruktiv feedback
* Tar ansvar och arbetar strukturerat


 Om arbetsplatsen
   

  

 Du blir en del av en verksamhet med högt fokus på arbetsmiljö, säkerhet och kvalitet. Anläggningen arbetar aktivt med att utveckla moderna och effektiva processer inom avfallshantering, med målet att bidra till en mer hållbar framtid.
   

  

 Om Simplex Bemanning
   

  

 Det är
 **Simplex Bemanning** 
 som ansvarar för hela rekryteringsprocessen och anställningen. Vi arbetar nära våra kunder och matchar rätt person till rätt uppdrag. Hos oss får du en trygg anställning, tydlig kommunikation och en arbetsgivare som bryr sig om din utveckling.
   

  

 Låter detta som något för dig?
   

  

 Skicka in din ansökan redan idag – urval sker löpande!

title: IT Service Lead
description:
**Om oss** 




 Tistel utvecklar och tillverkar obemannade farkoster i teknikens absoluta framkant, med fokus på att möta verkliga och växande behov. Vårt arbete sker i skärningspunkten mellan robotik, artificiell intelligens och högvolymsproduktion, där avancerad teknik omsätts i pålitliga och skalbara lösningar.
 



  





 Vi är ett ingenjörsdrivet bolag med höga ambitioner, korta beslutsvägar och en kultur där nyfikenhet, eftertanke och egna idéer uppmuntras. Här värdesätts förmågan att se helheten lika mycket som viljan att förstå detaljerna på djupet.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som IT Service Lead hos oss får du en central roll i att bygga den tekniska miljö som gör det möjligt för våra ingenjörsteam att arbeta snabbt, säkert och utan hinder. Du ansvarar inte bara för att systemen fungerar – utan för att skapa förutsättningarna för att vår teknikutveckling kan ske utan friktion.
 



  





 Du är vår förste dedikerade IT-resurs, vilket innebär stort handlingsutrymme men också att du behöver trivas med att sätta agendan själv och vara van att fatta beslut utan ett IT-team i ryggen.
 



  





 Eftersom vi är ett bolag i stark tillväxt innebär rollen stora möjligheter att påverka hur vår IT-plattform byggs upp, från utvecklingsmiljöer och arbetsverktyg till säkerhetsstruktur och systemarkitektur. Vi verkar i miljöer med höga säkerhetskrav, vilket innebär att du bidrar till lösningar som uppfyller regulatoriska krav – utan att bromsa utvecklingstakten.
 



  





**Erfarenheter vi tror att du har** 




 Vi tror att du har en gedigen bakgrund inom IT-drift, systemadministration eller IT-support och är van att arbeta nära utvecklingsteam.
 



  





 Mer konkret ser vi gärna att du har erfarenhet inom flera av följande:
 



  




* Windows klient- och servermiljöer
* Microsoft 365 / Active Directory / Entra ID
* Virtualisering (VMware eller Hyper-V)
* Nätverk (LAN, VLAN, VPN, brandväggar)
* IT-säkerhetsprinciper och säker informationshantering
* Versionshantering och utvecklingsverktyg (t.ex. Git)



  





 Det är meriterande om du även har arbetat med:
 



  




* IT-miljöer med höga säkerhetskrav, air-gapped eller isolerade nätverk
* ISO 27001 eller liknande säkerhetsramverk



  





**Personlighet och inställning vi letar efter** 




 Vi söker dig som trivs i en miljö där tempot är högt, mycket fortfarande formas och du har verkligt ansvar att påverka hur saker ska fungera.
 



  




* Du löser problem och tar initiativ för att få saker att fungera i praktiken
* Du har ett starkt service- och användarfokus och ser dig som ett stöd för teamet
* Du gillar att bygga struktur i miljöer som fortfarande utvecklas
* Du trivs med att äga hela IT-domänen själv och ser det som en möjlighet snarare än en begränsning
* Du håller dig proaktivt uppdaterad på nya säkerhetshot och sårbarheter – inklusive i hårdvara och trådlösa enheter – och agerar innan problemen uppstår
* Du är nyfiken på ny teknik och arbetar självständigt med god helhetssyn



  





**Det här erbjuder vi** 



* En möjlighet att bygga upp IT-miljön i ett teknikbolag på en stark tillväxtresa, där du får verkligt inflytande
* Nära samarbete med mycket erfarna ingenjörer i en teknisk miljö i framkanten
* En arbetsplats där innovation, säkerhet och samhällsnytta möts
* Konkurrenskraftiga villkor och en kultur som präglas av samarbete, respekt och inkludering



  





 Rollen är baserad på vårt helt nya kontor i centrala Stockholm.
 



  





 Av säkerhetsskäl kräver rollen att du har svenskt medborgarskap.
 



  





**För oss är rätt sammanhang lika viktigt som rätt kompetens. Om du vill bygga IT-miljön som gör vår teknikutveckling möjlig – välkommen med din ansökan!**

title: Projektledare digitalisering - hälso- och sjukvård
description:
**Jobbeskrivning**
 Hos Sopra Steria är du mer än en projektledare – du är en nyckelperson i utvecklingen av framtidens digitala hälso- och sjukvård. I denna roll arbetar du i gränslandet mellan verksamhet och IT, med fokus på att stödja våra kunder i deras digitaliseringsresa inom vård och omsorg.
   

  

 Du leder och driver uppdrag kopplade till införande, utveckling och förbättring av IT-stöd i komplexa vårdmiljöer. Rollen innebär nära samarbete med vårdverksamhet, IT-organisationer, leverantörer och andra intressenter, där din förmåga att skapa struktur, engagemang och framdrift är avgörande.
   

  

 Du blir en del av ett starkt team av konsulter med djup kompetens inom digitalisering av vård och omsorg, i en miljö som präglas av kunskapsdelning, kvalitet och samhällsnytta.
   

  

**Dina ansvarsområden**
* Leda och driva projekt och uppdrag inom digitalisering av hälso- och sjukvård
* Stötta kunder vid införande, uppgraderingar och förändringsarbete kopplat till IT-stöd
* Fungera som brygga mellan vårdverksamhet, IT och systemleverantörer
* Säkerställa att lösningar möter både verksamhetskrav och tekniska förutsättningar
* Planera, följa upp och rapportera leveranser enligt etablerade modeller
* Bidra med din erfarenhet i förbättrings- och utvecklingsinitiativ inom vårddigitalisering


**Om dig**
 Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av att arbeta inom hälso- och sjukvård och som trivs i en rådgivande och ledande roll nära kund, t.ex. som projektledare, applikationsspecialist, förvaltningsledare eller verksamhetsnära expert.
   

  

**Du Har Sannolikt**
* Deltagit i eller drivit förändrings- och/eller utvecklingsprojekt inom hälso- och sjukvården.
* God förståelse för vårdprocesser och informationsflöden
* Vana att arbeta i komplexa organisationer med många intressenter
* Förmåga att kommunicera tydligt och skapa förtroende på alla nivåer
* Relevant högskole- eller universitetsutbildning
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av arbete som konsult
* Kunskap i Cambios produktportfölj
* Erfarenhet av införande av systemstöd i större organisationer, t.ex. Cambios journalsystem Cosmic.
* Erfarenhet av förändringsledning eller agila arbetssätt
* Certifiering inom projektledning (t.ex. PMI, Prince2 eller motsvarande)


**Varför välja Sopra Steria?**
* En plats där din kompetens verkligen gör skillnad – Vi bygger smarta och hållbara lösningar som förbättrar samhälle och verksamheter med användarupplevelsen i centrum.
* Stark företagskultur med Power of Sharing – Vi tror på kraften i kunskapsdelning, transparens och samarbete. Hos oss når vi längre tillsammans.
* Toppbetyg som arbetsplats – Great Place to Work har rankat oss år efter år som en av Sveriges bästa arbetsplatser och vi har mottagit utmärkelsen Karriärföretag flera år i rad.
* Del av en europeisk ledare inom digital transformation – Med över 50 000 experter i många länder kan vi både påverka stora projekt och ge dig möjligheter att växa globalt.
* Värderingar som genomsyrar allt vi gör – Vi sätter kundens behov i fokus, prioriterar respekt, professionalitet, nyfikenhet och en kollektiv förmåga att agera positivt och skapa hållbara resultat tillsammans.


**Ansökan**
 Nu vill vi gärna höra lite om dig! Ansök enkelt genom att antingen ladda upp ditt CV eller länka direkt till ditt LinkedIn-konto via annonsen. Om det fortfarande finns något du funderar över är du välkommen att kontakta rekryterande chef Marie Berg, marie.berg@soprasteria.com eller Talent Acquisition Partner Svante Larsson, svante.larsson2@soprasteria.com
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att få höra ifrån dig!
   

  

 På Sopra Steria ställer vi höga krav på både kompetens och säkerhetsmedvetenhet hos våra medarbetare. Innan en eventuell anställning kommer du därför att genomgå en bakgrundskontroll, och vid intervju behöver du kunna uppvisa giltig legitimation.

title: Engineering Operations Lead - Tieto Caretech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
**At Tieto Caretech you’ll get a chance to provide patients and end users with purposeful technology and product offerings that help them in their life. If that sounds interesting, please keep on reading.**
**About Us**
 Through advanced, intuitive technology, Tieto Caretech reduces administrative work, improves decision-making, and strengthens safety and quality in health and social care. With the Lifecare software portfolio we enable health and social care professionals to focus on what matters most: people.
   

  

 We are a global team of professionals working on providing products and services to different sectors from intensive care to laboratory and social care. In addition to working closely with our customers, we also have our own products that we build and develop for various fields in society. From enabling healthcare professionals to spend more valuable time with their patients to providing data for professional athletes to achieve even better results, we make all that possible. With us you can be part of advancing societies through purposeful technology and make your mark on the health and wellbeing of people.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is a people-first leadership role with broad operational scope. You will lead the Caretech Engineering Excellence and Caretech Security teams across India and Nordic, with a strong focus on developing talent, strengthening capability, and enabling high performance. Success in this role is defined not by deep technical specialization, but by sound judgment, intellectual curiosity, and disciplined execution. In addition to people leadership, you will own a EUR 1–1.2M budget, lead cost optimization initiatives, govern tool processes, and represent the function across senior Caretech leadership, Finance, Procurement, and key external partners, including Microsoft. This role requires a leader who is organized, commercially astute, and effective in navigating a complex cross-functional stakeholder environment.
   

  

**In this role you will**
* Lead and develop a cross-regional team across Tooling, Infrastructure, and Security, with a strong focus on capability
* Own the Engineering Excellence budget of EUR 1–1.2M, ensuring strong financial governance, forecasting discipline, and cost transparency.
* Drive strategic cost optimization programs across software, licenses, tools, and cloud infrastructure, with clear savings targets and measurable outcomes.
* Manage key stakeholder and supplier relationships, including Microsoft, while leading procurement, renewals, and commercial negotiations with appropriate governance and compliance.
* Provide leadership across EE operational processes, ensuring risks, priorities, and execution are clearly governed, communicated, and followed through.


**Further More, Following Aspects Are Included In The Role**
* Broad leadership scope: The role combines people development, financial management, supplier negotiation, Tool /Infra governance oversight at the same time.
* High complexity and accountability: It operates across multiple country, a high paced work place, and a budget with clear performance targets.
* Sustained leadership demand: Success requires a leader who is consistently present, builds trust over time, and can manage upward, sideward, and within the team effectively.
* International travel to company offices will be expected.


**We are looking for someone with**
* Minimum 5 years proven people leadership experience in technology, engineering, or shared services environments, with a strong track record of developing junior and mid-level talent.
* 3-5 years experience in strong program leadership, with the ability to drive multiple priorities, align cross-functional stakeholders, and deliver against deadlines and financial targets.
* Commercial and financial acumen, including budget ownership, spend management, and accountability for cost optimization outcomes.
* Disciplined execution, sound judgment, and the curiosity to lead technical teams effectively without needing deep technical specialization.


**It would be beneficial if you also**
* Experience leading cross country distributed teams.
* Experience managing major technology suppliers, including licensing, renewals, and commercial negotiations.
* Familiarity with cost optimization, software asset management, or cloud spend governance.


**What’s in it for you?**
 With us you will be part of the important and meaningful work of developing healthcare for all of us. You have an important mission to make a difference that really matters. In addition, as a company we offer you professional growth, open and friendly culture, and an outstanding work-life balance! We believe that our organizational culture is an important part of enabling you to be successful. We provide a flexible hybrid work model as part of our culture and way of working.
   

  

 Finally, we also believe in curiosity and learning as a lifestyle. We want to encourage you to keep up that curiosity and deepen your knowledge on topics you’re interested in. For example, we host a ‘Keep Learning Week’ twice a year, a week where everyone at Tieto has the possibility to join different training courses in a truly global setting.
   

  

**Could you be our new colleague?**
 We look forward to hearing from you! We will fill the position as soon as we find the right person, so we encourage you to apply today. If you have any questions regarding this role, please contact the hiring manager Priya Tandon @ priya.tandon@tieto.com.
   

  

*We perform background checks on all final candidates.*
*Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.*
 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.
   

  

 Diversity, equity and inclusion | Sustainability | Tieto

title: Information Security Manager
description:
**Job Description**
 Information Security Manager
   

  

**Remote/Hybrid, Stockholm, Sweden**
 Do you want to work in a strategically important role where information security, regulatory compliance, IT security, and business value connect? We are now looking for an
 **Information Security Manager (ISM)** 
 who wants to take overall responsibility for information and IT security in complex customer and delivery environments.
   

  

 As an Information Security Manager, you are the central point of contact for security matters in customer engagements. The role combines governance, advisory, and follow-up, ensuring that both the customer's and the organization's security requirements, standards, and contractual obligations are met.
   

  

 You will work closely with customers, delivery teams, Service Delivery Management, Security Operations Center (SOC), and other key functions. The role is non-operational but requires a strong technical understanding and a high level of security maturity.
   

  

 As Our Future Information Security Manager , You Will
   

  

* Be the single point of contact for security in customer engagements
* Ensure compliance with information security requirements, agreements, and internal standards
* Establish, manage, and follow up on the information security risk register
* Lead and participate in various levels of security forums with our customers and conduct security reviews
* Prepare and present monthly security reporting
* Contribute to and maintain development plans from a security perspective
* Support during security incidents, investigations, and escalations
* Review security aspects in changes (Change Assessment)
* Ensure that architecture and design documents are reviewed from a security perspective
* Create, manage, and follow up on delivery-specific security handbooks and plans.
* Collaborate with sales and identify business opportunities within security


**Your profile**
 You are proactive, structured, and analytical, with a strong sense of responsibility.
   

  

 You have got several years of experience in information security work and several years of experience in IT security, preferably in a technical role.
   

  

 You possess both practical and theoretical knowledge of ISMS, risk management, incident management, and compliance follow‑up. You have experience working closely with customers in complex IT and operational environments and the ability to work in a structured, independent, and advisory manner. You also have excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English, spoken and written, and you have documented experience in leading security work at an account level or equivalent.
   

  

 It is considered a strong merit if you have experience of working with established frameworks and standards, and have experience from managed services or larger outsourcing contracts.
   

  

**What is in it for you?**
* A business ‑ critical and visible role with significant trust and responsibility
* The opportunity to influence and develop security work for major customers
* Collaboration with experienced specialists in security, operations, and architecture
* A flexible way of working with possibility to work remotely part time
* A professional environment where information security is a strategic priority


**Shape your world and achieve together**
 We want the best people on our team, so we welcome and encourage applications from people with a diverse variety of experiences, backgrounds, and identities. We are committed to equality of opportunity for all. If you want to work alongside people with different ideas, in an inclusive business that will welcome you and support you, come and co-create the future at Fujitsu.
   

  

**About Fujitsu**
 Since being founded in Japan in 1935, we have continued to demonstrate our technological prowess and the persistent pursuit of innovation. As a world-leading digital transformation partner, our business structure is aligned with the modern digital world. We use our experience and the power of IT to shape the future of society with our customers.
   

  

***For the consultant/employee selected for the role, checks of public registers and records relating to financial and legal status will be conducted. By submitting an application, the candidate consents to such checks being carried out.***

title: Sales Consultant
description:
**About Mediaplanet**
 We Create Impact!
   

  

 Mediaplanet is a world leader in content marketing, creating powerful campaigns together with 15,000+ clients globally. We make branding easy by managing the entire value chain – from concept and production to distribution and collaborations with influencers and world-renowned media houses. As part of Caybon, we have 10 offices across Europe and North America.
   

  

***We are now looking for a Sales Consultant***
**About The Role**
 As a Sales Consultant in Sweden, you are responsible for the funding of your campaign and the topic from the idea stage all the way to release date.
   

  

 You will start every quarter with in-depth research about a relevant topic and current trends within a selected industry. You will identify the most important influencers and experts while securing the best possible distribution channels. When funding your campaign, you must pitch the campaign and the Mediaplanet products to the right clients.
   

  

 To be successful in the sales process, it is paramount that you have a thorough understanding of the client’s needs and that you are able to identify which products in our portfolio will match the expectations and goals for the client’s participation in the campaign. Whilst keeping a high pace and focus on bringing in revenue.
   

  

**Your Profile**
* You are curious and hungry to learn about Sales within the Media Industry
* 1-2 years of B2B sales experience
* High drive – Goal-oriented with high energy and thrives in a fast-paced environment
* You like to work in a young and vibrant environment at an office in central Stockholm
* You might have experience from a similar sales job and/or the media industry
* A university degree is preferred but not required – we value the right mindset and experience over formal education.
* Fluency in Swedish is a must.


**Why Mediaplanet?**
 High Degree of Freedom & Impact - You have the opportunity to create impact for clients, yourself and the company.
   

  

 Work in an innovative environment where you can create unique campaigns and collaborations.
   

  

 We have a strong entrepreneurial culture and provide our employees with opportunities to grow and develop.
   

  

 Mediaplanet delivers top class content marketing campaigns together with 15 000+ clients globally. Since the start in Stockholm back in 2002, we have grown into the world’s leading content marketing group with 15 offices across Europe and the North Americas.

title: Major Account Manager
description:
**Our Mission**
 At Palo Alto Networks®, we’re united by a shared mission—to protect our digital way of life. We thrive at the intersection of innovation and impact, solving real-world problems with cutting-edge technology and bold thinking. Here, everyone has a voice, and every idea counts. If you’re ready to do the most meaningful work of your career alongside people who are just as passionate as you are, you’re in the right place.
   

  

**Who We Are**
 In order to be the cybersecurity partner of choice, we must trailblaze the path and shape the future of our industry. This is something our employees work at each day and is defined by our values: Disruption, Collaboration, Execution, Integrity, and Inclusion. We weave AI into the fabric of everything we do and use it to augment the impact every individual can have. If you are passionate about solving real-world problems and ideating beside the best and the brightest, we invite you to join us!
   

  

 This role is remote, but distance is no barrier to impact. Our hybrid teams collaborate across geographies to solve big problems, stay close to our customers, and grow together. You will be part of a culture that values trust, accountability, and shared success where your work truly matters.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Major Account Manager partners with our customers to secure their entire digital experience. \* You’re motivated by the desire to solve critical challenges facing our customer’s secure environment, so you’re prepared to connect them with a solution for every stage of threat prevention. This role is a significant driver of company revenue and growth. As an experienced and dynamic sales professional, you’re responsible for leading and driving sales engagements.
   

  

 Palo Alto Networks Is Leading The Charge In Platformization, Offering Best-in-breed Solutions That Enable Customers To Build a Truly Zero-trust Security Architecture And Navigate Critical Transformations. To Ensure Our Sales Team Is Equipped To Guide Customers, We've Developed FLIGHT, An Immersive Onboarding Program
   

  

 Flight blends virtual and in-person learning at our headquarters, where new sales hires will participate in dynamic cohorts, fully dedicated to their training without customer distractions.
   

  

 This focused approach ensures they emerge as well-prepared sales professionals, ready to help customers leverage our comprehensive portfolio.
   

  

 Your Impact
   

  

* As a Major Account Manager, you will drive and orchestrate large complex sales cycles and work with our internal partners and teams to best serve the customer.
* Your consultative selling experience will identify business challenges and create solutions for prospects and our customers.
* Understand the competitive landscape and customer needs so you can effectively position the portfolio of Palo Alto Networks solutions.
* Create clear goals and complete accurate forecasting through developing a detailed territory plan.
* Leverage prospect stories to create a compelling value proposition with insights into value for that specific account.
* Stay updated on industry news and trends, and how they affect Palo Alto Networks products and services.
* Identifies problems, reviews data, determines the root causes, and provides scalable solutions
* Cultivate relationships with our channel partners to bring channel-centric go-to-market approach for our customers
* Travel as necessary within your territory, and to company-wide meetings.


**Required**
**Qualifications**
 Minimum 15 years experience including 6 years in large Enterprise SaaS or IT Security companies
   

  

**Minimum 15 Years Enterprise Sales Experience**
 Fluent Swedish
   

  

**Preferred**
 Experience and knowledge of SaaS-based architectures, ideally in a networking and/or security industry.
   

  

 Demonstrated experience selling complex solutions, value selling, and/or consultative sales techniques Technical aptitude for understanding how technology products and solutions solve business problems
   

  

 Demonstrates in depth knowledge of the full sales cycle and the ability to follow a structured sales process Ability to take a holistic approach to problem solving by understanding the bigger picture, and considering complex interrelationships and outcomes Excellent time management skills, and work with high levels of autonomy and self-direction
   

  

**Our Commitment**
 We’re trailblazers that dream big, take risks, and challenge cybersecurity’s status quo. It’s simple: we can’t accomplish our mission without diverse teams innovating, together.
   

  

 We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for all qualified individuals with a disability. If you require assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need, please contact us at accommodations@paloaltonetworks.com.
   

  

 Palo Alto Networks is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity in our workplace, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or other legally protected characteristics.
   

  

 All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Director HR Operations- Europe
description:
Lyten is leading an industrial revolution through Lyten 3D Graphene™, a breakthrough supermaterial unlocking a new generation of products — from lithium–sulfur batteries and energy storage systems to concrete admixtures, lightweight composites, and next-generation sensors that are revolutionizing industries. Together, these innovations are making a massive global improvement and driving real-world impact across energy, mobility, construction, and defense.
   

  

 At Lyten, we believe the most meaningful careers begin with purpose — and with people who want to make a difference. We’re not just developing advanced supermaterials — we’re about to change the world as we know it, reshaping how energy is stored, how products are built, and how progress is made.
   

  

 We’re entering an exciting growth phase, scaling production across the U.S. and Europe and expanding our team of engineers, scientists, and innovators.
   

  

 Apply now to join our team and be part of something bigger than yourself — where collaboration, creativity, and purpose come together to build the technologies that will define the next century.
   

  

**Director, HR Operations Europe**
**Responsibilities**
**HR Operations and Process Excellence**
* Oversee all HR operations including employee administration, HRIS management, and payroll coordination.
* Design and implement standardized HR processes across sites, ensuring compliance with Swedish employment law, GDPR, and company policy.
* Monitor key HR metrics (turnover, absenteeism, headcount, cost per hire) and report trends to management.
* Lead continuous improvement projects to streamline HR workflows and enhance employee experience.


**Operations**
* Administration of HR systems & applications
* Data input & management
* HR project management as needed


**Mobility**
* VISA & immigration management
* Global mobility programs & admin
* Vendor management


**Hris**
* Leading integration activities from historical HRIS to WD
* Optimize & process improve system architecture
* Project management for talent programs
* HR metrics, scorecards & ad hoc reports
* 3rd party application integrations


**Payroll**
* Leading monthly manual payroll processing efforts
* Payroll vendor Management
* Own implementation of new global payroll vendor
* Implementation of new timekeeping tool in Workday for European locations


**Required Skills**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
* Minimum 8–10 years of HR experience, with at least 3 years in HR operations or HR service delivery leadership.
* Proficiency in HRIS and data management (e.g., Workday, SAP, SuccessFactors, UKG or equivalent).


**Preferred Skills**
* Strong understanding of Swedish labor law, collective agreements, and payroll processes.
* Experience in manufacturing or industrial environments preferred.
* Fluent in English and Swedish (spoken and written).


**Why Work at Lyten**
 At Lyten, you’ll be part of a team that’s redefining what’s possible in energy, materials, and manufacturing. We’re not just imagining the future — we’re building it today with breakthrough technologies that are changing how products are made and how industries innovate.
   

  

 Our people are the heart of that mission. From world-class scientists and engineers to creative problem-solvers in operations, manufacturing, and commercialization, every member of the Lyten team plays a role in turning bold ideas into real-world impact. We believe that the best work happens when you’re doing great things in the world — with people you like. Collaboration, curiosity, and a shared sense of purpose drive everything we do.
   

  

 What You’ll Find at Lyten
   

  

* A mission that matters: Contribute directly to solving complex challenges in energy, mobility, and materials innovation.
* Cutting-edge innovation: Work on technologies at the intersection of materials science, energy storage, and advanced manufacturing that strengthen energy security and local supply chains.
* Extraordinary people: Join a team of talented, friendly, and down-to-earth innovators who support, challenge, and inspire one another every day.
* Teamwork and culture: Experience a workplace built on trust, respect, and shared success — where collaboration fuels breakthroughs and everyone’s ideas are heard.
* Global impact: Help scale new materials and energy solutions that reinforce industrial resilience across the U.S. and Europe.
* Career growth: Be part of a fast-moving company entering a commercial growth phase, with opportunities to lead, learn, and make your mark.
* Purpose-driven values: Thrive in an environment that celebrates ingenuity, optimism, and meaningful progress — together.


 Lyten offers the opportunity to do the most important work of your career — helping build the technologies that will power the next century of innovation.
   

  

 Join us, and help transform industries, communities, and the planet with friends who share your drive to make a difference.

title: GDPR Operational Lead
description:
**Job Description**
 The GDPR Operational Lead plays a central role in ensuring customers experience GDPR compliance as an integrated and reliable part of service delivery. By translating regulatory requirements into day‑to‑day operational controls, the role strengthens customer confidence and long‑term partnerships.
   

  

 The GDPR Operational role is responsible for ensuring that GDPR requirements are implemented, operated and followed in day ‑ to ‑ day service delivery.
   

  

 The role bridges legal data protection requirements and practical service operations, ensuring that personal data processing within delivered services is compliant, controlled and auditable.
   

  

 This is an operational role within Service Delivery, working closely with Information Security team, the Data Protection Officer (DPO), account teams and customers.
   

  

**As Our Future GDPR Operational Lead, You Will**
* Ensuring day ‑ to ‑ day compliant processing of personal data within delivered services
* Maintain and operate data handling routines, such as access control, logging, data retention and deletion
* Ensure GDPR considerations are embedded in service onboarding, transition and steady ‑ state operations
* Acting as SPOC for GDPR questions in Sweden delivery
* Supporting audits and customer assurance
* Coordinating incident handling for personal data breaches
* Maintaining documentation and system inventories relevant to personal data
* Working in close cooperation with Information Security Management (ISM) and DPO


**Your profile**
 You are proactive, structured, and analytical, with a strong sense of responsibility and you are as a person solution oriented.
   

  

**You Have**
* Solid experience from IT Service Delivery, Operations or Security
* Practical understanding of GDPR in an operational context (beyond policy and legal interpretation)
* Lead and coordinated daily operational GDPR actvities
* Experience working with:
+ Service management and delivery models
+ Information security controls, privacy incidents and incident response
+ Customer assurance, audits or compliance reviews
+ Operational GDPR activities such as data handling routines, risk assessment or cross-functional coordination
+ Ability to work cross‑functionally and act as a trusted operational advisor


 You have experience from managed services, outsourcing or complex IT environments. You have knowledge of and experience with ITIL, ISO/IEC 27001, ISAE, and NIS2-related requirements. You have experience interacting with customers on compliance security topics.
   

  

 The role requires background checks, and Swedish citizenship, and potentially also security clearance depending on customer need.
   

  

**What is in it for you?**
* A business ‑ critical and visible role with significant trust and responsibility
* The opportunity to influence and develop data privacy work for major customers
* A flexible way of working with possibility to work remotely part time

title: Production Planner
description:

****Who we are:****
 Crane Currency is a premier industrial technology company and a pioneer in currency manufacturing and advanced micro-optics technology for securing physical products. Yes, chances are you have our product in your wallet right now! Our sophisticated equipment and software leverage proprietary core capabilities with detection and sensing technologies. Around the world, our customers trust us to anticipate their needs with innovative, reliable, and high-quality currency & authentication solutions and services—just as they have for generations.  Learn more at www.cranecurrency.com
   

  

**How You Will Make An Impact**
 As a Production Planning Engineer at Crane Currency, you will play a crucial role in enhancing our processes and ensuring high-quality materials are ready for production. You will be responsible for maintaining the production schedule, identifying and mitigating potential issues, and ensuring timely delivery to our customers. Your role involves efficient and stable planning of banknote paper production in Tumba, aligning the requirements of Customers, Management, Factory, and Finance. Both short-term and long-term planning, along with follow-ups, are essential to ensure consistency and efficiency.
   

  

**In Your Role You Will**

* Understand the requirements of the Bid process to make sure the promises to customers can later be met
* Follow up production results and publish reports daily, weekly and monthly.
* Participate in work to develop tools for planning and countdown to automate the process within Tumba and together with other sites.


****Knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:****
****Required:****
* University degree in Engineering or Business Administration.
* Minimum 3 years working experience with Production planning, preferably in a distributed environment.
* Experience working in logistics.
* Documented leadership skills (formal / informal / projects)

title: Finance Solutions Lead
description:
We are on a mission to become the leading international force in residential property management. What we do matters to billions of people in their everyday lives.
   

  

 Residential real estate is the world’s largest asset class, and for most families, buying a home is the single biggest financial decision they will ever make. For too long, residential property management has fallen short of homeowners' and residents' expectations, and the industry is ready for change. Odevo was formed to make that change happen.
   

  

 In just six years, Odevo has grown more than 70-fold, establishing us as the leading international player. We are more than 12,000 people across the US, the UK, the Nordics, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Mexico, and Italy building Odevo. Together, we aspire to break new ground through innovation and cultivate a workplace where we help each other succeed, where ideas matter more than titles, and where kindness is a strength.
   

  

 By combining the power of people and technology, we set a new standard for how homes are managed. Our technology-enabled services simplify living for residents and create a better experience for property owners and boards.
   

  

 Our ambition is to continue our fast-growing journey through new customers, service expansion, and by partnering with great companies that share our mindset and ambition, both in existing and new markets.
   

  

 As we continue to grow, we stay true to who we are, challenge unnecessary bureaucracy, and keep decision-making close to our customers.
   

  

 If you share our mindset and ambition, we’d love you to join us and help accelerate our mission together.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As our finance operations scale, so does the complexity of the systems and controls that underpin them. We are now building the function that bridges finance and technology — and this role sits at the center of it
   

  

 As Finance Solutions Lead, you are the link between Finance and IT Operations at Odevo. You own two things: making sure the controls, processes, and governance frameworks across our finance systems are properly designed and functioning — and leading the consolidation of our ERP landscape onto Business Central, market by market.
   

  

 This is not a purely technical role, nor a purely finance one. You need to understand financial processes well enough to design the right controls, and understand systems well enough to implement and govern them. You will work across Group Finance, IT Operations, Financial Control, and local opco teams, and you report to the Head of Group Financial Control, with a dotted line to the SVP IT Operations.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
*Finance-IT governance and controls*
* Own the interface between Finance and IT Operations: ensure finance system processes, access controls, and change management routines are properly designed, documented, and operating effectively.
* Define and maintain IT General Controls across finance systems — covering access management (SoD, role design, MFA), change control, and system availability — for both compliance and operational integrity.
* Bring a clear point of view on how AI should shape finance system design going forward — from how controls are configured and monitored, to how the Business Central product roadmap evolves. We are not looking for enthusiasm about AI in the abstract; we want someone who can translate it into concrete decisions about what we build, what we automate, and where human judgment stays in the loop.
* Support SOX readiness and broader internal control frameworks as Odevo's compliance requirements evolve; work with Financial Control and external auditors as needed.
* Maintain a control register for finance systems; identify gaps, drive remediation, and track status across markets.
* Collaborate with IT Operations to ensure finance requirements are reflected in infrastructure decisions, security policies, and vendor management.


*IT service management*
* Act as formal system owner for Business Central: maintain system documentation and configuration records, manage the user license estate, and represent the system in IT governance forums and audit reviews.
* Own the review and approval process for Business Central release waves and hotfixes — scope what changes are applied, coordinate user acceptance testing with finance stakeholders, and enforce change freeze periods around month-end and period close.
* Own and maintain the Business Central product roadmap: work with Group Finance, local opco teams, and the implementation partner to prioritize enhancements, manage the development backlog, and communicate planned changes and timelines to stakeholders across the group.
* Chair regular IT-Finance service review meetings: monitor support ticket volumes and resolution times against agreed SLAs, act as the finance-side escalation point for production issues, and drive problem management for recurring system failures.
* Serve as the finance-side contact for major incidents affecting finance systems -coordinate the finance response during outages, participate in post-incident reviews, and track remediation actions to completion.
* Manage the relationship with the Business Central implementation and support partner: review delivery against statements of work, conduct regular performance reviews, and ensure the partner roadmap aligns with Odevo’s priorities.
* Represent Finance in IT steering and planning forums, ensuring finance system priorities are incorporated into the technology roadmap ahead of annual budgeting and resource allocation cycles.


*ERP consolidation program*
* Lead the rollout of Business Central across Odevo's operating companies — scoping, configuration, data migration, testing, training, and go-live.
* Define and steward the group ERP template: core process design, and configuration standards that balance group consistency with local operational realities.
* Manage rollouts across multiple markets in parallel, coordinating local finance teams, Group Technology, and external implementation partners.
* Maintain system documentation and process description
* Application owner BC
* Integrate acquired companies onto Business Central post-close, working with the M&A team to assess legacy landscapes and plan transitions.
* Manage integrations between Business Central and adjacent tools including AARO, FloQast and our internal Odevo Datawarehouse.


**Who You Are**
 You are equally at home in a controls review conversation and an ERP configuration session. You understand why segregation of duties matters and how to enforce it in Business Central. You can run a project, manage competing priorities across markets, and bring skeptical local teams along with you.
   

  

 You are the kind of person who notices when the Finance-IT boundary creates a gap — and closes it before it becomes a problem. You communicate clearly across functions, translate between finance language and technical language, and build trust with stakeholders who have different priorities and incentives.
   

  

**Required Qualifications**
* Proven experience with Business Central (D365 BC) — implementation, configuration, or system ownership.
* Solid understanding of IT General Controls: access management, change control, SoD, and how these are designed and tested in a finance systems context.
* Strong grounding in core financial processes — period close, AP, AR, consolidation — and how they translate into system controls and configuration.
* Demonstrated project management capability across multi-entity or multi-market environments.

title: Azure Cloud Engineer
description:
Vill du ta din expertis inom Azure till nästa nivå? Vi söker en person med erfarenhet inom Microsoft Cloud som vill vara med och designa, bygga och optimera framtidens molnlösningar tillsammans med oss på Accelerate. Hos oss får du inte bara arbeta med moderna tekniker och spännande uppdrag – du blir en del av ett värderingsdrivet och passionerat team där du får goda möjligheter till personlig utveckling.
   

  

**Varför Accelerate?**
 Vi erbjuder dig mer än ett uppdrag – vi erbjuder en plattform för att växa som expert inom Microsoft Cloud så som Azure Infrastruktur, Azure DevOps och andra Microsoft Cloud lösningar! Hos oss får du chansen att arbeta i en modern och framtidsinriktad molnmiljö tillsammans med erfarna kollegor som är Microsoft MVP och Microsoft Certified Trainer inom Azure. Här blir du en del av en kultur där vi lär av varandra och bygger lösningar som verkligen gör skillnad hos våra kunder.
   

  

**Som Konsult Hos Oss Får Du**
* En skräddarsydd utvecklings- och certifieringsplan
* Utmanande och varierande uppdrag med de senaste teknikerna
* Flexibilitet, frihet och balans mellan jobb och fritid
* Kollektivavtal, friskvårdsbidrag och sjuk- och hälsoförsäkring
* Ett sammansvetsat team av vassa specialister som lyfter varandra
* En trygg och säker anställning med bra förmåner


 Vi tror på gemenskap och kompetensutbyte – här utvecklas vi tillsammans och stöttar varandra för att alltid ligga i framkant.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Azure Cloud Engineer hos oss är du en nyckelspelare i att skapa stabila, säkra och moderna molnplattformar åt våra kunder. Du blir motorn bakom en friktionsfri resa från kod till produktion genom att automatisera, effektivisera och optimera kundernas Azure-miljöer. Vi ser dig som den som frigör kraften i molnet, så att utvecklingsteamen kan fokusera på innovation.
   

  

**Exempel På Vad Du Kommer Att Göra**
* Bygga och underhålla en modern, säker och skalbar infrastruktur i Azure med Infrastructure as Code.
* Hantera lösningar inom PaaS (Platform as a Service) och IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service), inklusive Azure VM, Azure Virtual Desktop, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Azure Container Apps m.m.
* Säkerställa governance och compliance genom att implementera och hantera Azure Policy, RBAC och Workload Identity Federation.
* Optimera och implementera effektiva lösningar för monitorering och loggning med hjälp av Azure Monitor, Log Analytics och Application Insights.
* Säkerställa hög tillgänglighet och lösa komplexa utmaningar inom nätverk och säkerhet i Azure.


**Vem är du?**
 Vi söker dig som är har gedigen erfarenhet som Cloud Engineer och som brinner för Cloud lösningar såsom Azure, automation och säkerhet. Du har ett lösningsfokuserat mindset och vill hjälpa våra kunder att bygga hållbara och effektiva molnlösningar.
   

  

 Du uttrycker dig obehindrat på svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift. För denna position krävs svenskt medborgarskap, vi utför även bakgrundskontroll av slutkandidat.
   

  

**Vi Tror Att Du Gillar Att Jobba Med**
* Cloud Infrastruktur: Azure VM, Azure Network, Azure Virtual Desktop, Azure Kubernetes Services
* Azure-tjänster som Function Apps, App Services, Key Vault och Storage Accounts
* Säkerhet och compliance: Entra ID, Managed Identities, RBAC, Azure Policy, Microsoft Security
* Monitorering och loggning: Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights
* Infrastructure as Code: som t ex Bicep
* Erfarenhet av att bygga cloudmiljöer enligt CAF (Cloud Adotions Framework) eller liknande.


**Plus Om Du Har**
* Certifieringar som AZ-104 och/eller AZ-204
* Erfarenhet av: Azure Landing Zones
* AZ-400, AZ-305 eller motsvarande


**Placeringsort**
 Accelerate finns på flertal orter i Sverige och Norge. För denna tjänst utgår du från vårt kontor i Stockholm, med möjlighet till hybridarbete.
   

  

**Vi intervjuar löpande – vänta inte med att skicka in din ansökan!**
 Önskad start är snarast eller enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

 Har du frågor? Kontakta Jesper Carlsson på jesper.carlsson@iver.se
   

  

**Vi ser fram emot att träffa dig! Välkommen till Accelerate**

title: Databricks Champion
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.
   

  

**Your role**
 We are now seeking a passionate Databricks Expert/Champion or someone to growing into that space to join us. In this role, you will play an important role in shaping and contributing to Capgemini’s data, analytics & AI strategy forward using the Databricks platform. You will work hands-on with the latest tools in data ingestion, ETL, orchestration, and machine learning, while also contributing to solution design, architecture, and customer engagement. This is a role for someone who thrives at the intersection of technology, strategy, and communication.
   

  

 Capgemini has a strong and close collaboration with Databricks and was named EMEA Partner of the Year 2024 & 2025 – a recognition of our customer growth, joint engagements, and technical excellence. As part of our team, you’ll help strengthen this partnership and guide organizations in unlocking the full potential of the Databricks platform.
   

  

 We know that great candidates don’t always meet every requirement. If you’re interested in the role, we encourage you to apply even if your experience doesn’t match every point listed.
   

  

*In this role you will play a key part in:*
* Design and implement high-performance data pipelines using Databricks and Apache Spark
* Build and maintain secure data lakehouses and data warehouses in Azure
* Integrate AI/ML models into data pipelines using Databricks ML and Azure ML
* Lead or contributing to solutioning and architecture efforts – from whiteboard to production
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams including data scientists, analysts, and DevOps engineers
* Participate in presales, technical workshops, and customer presentations


**Your profile**
 We see this role being a great fit for someone with experience as a data engineer or architect with a passion for building scalable, high-impact data solutions. You combine deep technical skills with strong communication and solutioning capabilities.
   

  

*You have:*
* Proven experience designing and implementing data platforms using Databricks, Apache Spark, SQL and Python
* Several years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure, including services like Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Data Factory, and Azure ML
* A solid understanding of data architecture, including lakehouse design, data modeling and governance
* Experience working in presales, technical workshops and customer-facing presentations
* A collaborative mindset and the ability to translate business needs into technical solutions


**Databricks Champion – or on your way there**
*We are looking for someone who is either:*
* An official Databricks Champion, recognized for their technical excellence and community contributions, or
* A high-level Databricks expert with the ambition and potential to become a Champion. With Capgemini’s support, network, and close partnership with Databricks, we’ll help you take that next step.


**What We Believe You Will Enjoy About Working Here**
* We recognize the significance of flexible work arrangements to provide support. Be it remote work, or flexible work hours, you will get an environment to maintain healthy work life balance.
* At the heart of our mission is your career growth. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professions are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
* We partner with names such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Databricks, which can equip you with valuable certifications in the latest technologies in line with your ambitions.


**Application**
*As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace and welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We particularly encourage women and underrepresented groups in tech to apply.*
**About Capgemini**
 Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.
   

  

**Make it real** 
 | www.capgemini.com

title: Engineering Operations Lead - Tieto Caretech (m/f/d)
description:
Solna, Sweden


* Full-time
* Business Support
* Hybrid
* Tieto Caretech

  
Job Description **At Tieto Caretech you’ll get a chance to provide patients and end users with purposeful technology and product offerings that help them in their life. If that sounds interesting, please keep on reading.**

 **About us**


Through advanced, intuitive technology, Tieto Caretech reduces administrative work, improves decision-making, and strengthens safety and quality in health and social care. With the Lifecare software portfolio we enable health and social care professionals to focus on what matters most: people.


We are a global team of professionals working on providing products and services to different sectors from intensive care to laboratory and social care. In addition to working closely with our customers, we also have our own products that we build and develop for various fields in society. From enabling healthcare professionals to spend more valuable time with their patients to providing data for professional athletes to achieve even better results, we make all that possible. With us you can be part of advancing societies through purposeful technology and make your mark on the health and wellbeing of people.  



**About the Role**


This is a people-first leadership role with broad operational scope. You will lead the Caretech Engineering Excellence and Caretech Security teams across India and Nordic, with a strong focus on developing talent, strengthening capability, and enabling high performance. Success in this role is defined not by deep technical specialization, but by sound judgment, intellectual curiosity, and disciplined execution. In addition to people leadership, you will own a EUR 1–1.2M budget, lead cost optimization initiatives, govern tool processes, and represent the function across senior Caretech leadership, Finance, Procurement, and key external partners, including Microsoft. This role requires a leader who is organized, commercially astute, and effective in navigating a complex cross-functional stakeholder environment.

 **In this role you will**

* **Lead and develop a cross-regional team** across Tooling, Infrastructure, and Security, with a strong focus on capability
* **Own the Engineering Excellence budget** of EUR 1–1.2M, ensuring strong financial governance, forecasting discipline, and cost transparency.
* **Drive strategic cost optimization programs** across software, licenses, tools, and cloud infrastructure, with clear savings targets and measurable outcomes.
* **Manage key stakeholder and supplier relationships**, including Microsoft, while leading procurement, renewals, and commercial negotiations with appropriate governance and compliance.
* **Provide leadership across EE operational processes**, ensuring risks, priorities, and execution are clearly governed, communicated, and followed through.

**Further more, following aspects are included in the role:**

* **Broad leadership scope:** The role combines people development, financial management, supplier negotiation, Tool /Infra governance oversight at the same time.
* **High complexity and accountability:** It operates across multiple country, a high paced work place, and a budget with clear performance targets.
* **Sustained leadership demand:** Success requires a leader who is consistently present, builds trust over time, and can manage upward, sideward, and within the team effectively.
* **International travel** to company offices will be expected.

 **We are looking for someone with**

* Minimum 5 years proven people leadership experience in technology, engineering, or shared services environments, with a strong track record of developing junior and mid-level talent.
* 3-5 years experience in strong program leadership, with the ability to drive multiple priorities, align cross-functional stakeholders, and deliver against deadlines and financial targets.
* Commercial and financial acumen, including budget ownership, spend management, and accountability for cost optimization outcomes.
* Disciplined execution, sound judgment, and the curiosity to lead technical teams effectively without needing deep technical specialization.

 **It would be beneficial if you also**

* Experience leading cross country distributed teams.
* Experience managing major technology suppliers, including licensing, renewals, and commercial negotiations.
* Familiarity with cost optimization, software asset management, or cloud spend governance.

 **What’s in it for you?**


With us you will be part of the important and meaningful work of developing healthcare for all of us. You have an important mission to make a difference that really matters. In addition, as a company we offer you professional growth, open and friendly culture, and an outstanding work-life balance! We believe that our organizational culture is an important part of enabling you to be successful. We provide a flexible hybrid work model as part of our culture and way of working.


Finally, we also believe in curiosity and learning as a lifestyle. We want to encourage you to keep up that curiosity and deepen your knowledge on topics you’re interested in. For example, we host a ‘Keep Learning Week’ twice a year, a week where everyone at Tieto has the possibility to join different training courses in a truly global setting.

 **Could you be our new colleague?**


We look forward to hearing from you! We will fill the position as soon as we find the right person, so we encourage you to apply today. If you have any questions regarding this role, please contact the hiring manager Priya Tandon @ priya.tandon@tieto.com.

 *We perform background checks on all final candidates.*

*Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.*

  

Additional Information  

At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.


Diversity, equity and inclusion | Sustainability | Tieto


  
We provide customers across different industries with mission-critical solutions through our specialized software businesses Tieto Caretech, Tieto Banktech and Tieto Indtech as well as Tieto Tech Consulting business.


Our around 14 000 talented vertical software, design, cloud and AI experts are dedicated to empowering our customers to succeed and innovate with latest technology.


Tieto’s annual revenue is approximately EUR 2 billion. The company’s shares are listed on the NASDAQ exchange in Helsinki and Stockholm, as well as on Oslo Børs.

title: Integration Test & Validation Engineer
description:
Job Description
  
We are looking for an Integration Test & Validation Engineer to join a growing technical team in Gothenburg. This position will be responsible for providing overall services and test support to our SE Site in terms of TSP infrastructure, functional capabilities, and information security.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
* Validate system interactions and data consistency between:


o TSP backend services (Microservices)
  
o Vehicle side (TCU/TCAM/In-Vehicle Infotainment)
  
o Mobile applications (iOS/Android)
  
* Actively query and verify databases (SQL/NoSQL) to ensure data integrity, correct state transitions, and accurate business logic flow during integration.
* Analyze backend logs and APM tools (e.g., Kibana, Splunk) to identify root causes of business errors, communication failures, and API mismatches.
* Investigate data flow and communication between systems to ensure correct orchestration and messaging.
* Design and execute integration test scenarios covering real-world use cases (e.g., remote commands, vehicle status, OTA, eCall, and remote diagnostics).
* Support defect analysis and troubleshooting, bridging the gap between frontend, backend, and vehicle teams to drive complex cross-functional issues to closure.
* Collaborate closely with backend developers, vehicle teams, and app teams in an international environment.
* Verify API behaviors, message flows, and communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP/HTTPS, Protobuf).
* Prepare test reports, validation documentation, and quality assessments for release readiness.


Requirements & Technical Proficiency
  
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Telecommunications, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
* Minimum 3 years of experience in integration testing, joint debugging, or system validation, preferably in the telematics or connected vehicle domain.
* Deep understanding of connected car business logic and architecture (TSP / TCAM / Mobile App interaction flow).
* Proficient in database operations, with the ability to write SQL queries independently (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, or Oracle) for data verification and troubleshooting.
* Proven experience in log analysis and issue investigation across distributed systems (e.g., tracing a remote command failure from App, TSP, Vehicle).
* Solid knowledge of networking and communication protocols: TCP/IP, HTTP/HTTPS, MQTT, or other messaging queues.
* Familiarity with API testing tools (e.g., Postman, JMeter, curl) and log management tools (e.g., ELK stack, Kibana). Preferred Qualifications• Experience within OEM or Tier-1 automotive telematics projects (e.g., handling T-Box/TCU registration, activation, or TSP lifecycle management).
* Familiarity with common backend business error codes and exception handling mechanisms.
* Basic scripting skills (Python, Shell) for log parsing, data preparation, or test automation support.
* Experience with cloud-based backend systems (e.g., AWS, Azure) and microservices architecture. Personal attributes• Strong analytical mindset with a structured, end-to-end problem-solving approach.
* Excellent communication and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively drive joint- debugging sessions across different technical teams.
* Proactive, self-driven, and highly quality-oriented.
* Fluent in English (written and spoken) for daily collaboration in international teams.


What We Offer
  
A supportive and learning-oriented work environment
  
Opportunity to gain hands-on experience in IT operations within the automotive domain
  
Career development path towards IT Manager or other IT roles
  
Competitive salary and benefits in accordance with Swedish standards
  
Öppen för alla
  
Vi fokuserar på din kompetens, inte dina övriga förutsättningar. Vi är öppna för att anpassa rollen eller arbetsplatsen efter dina behov.

title: Enterprise Architect
description:
The key word is
 **change** 
 . Technology is no longer a support function. Agents are becoming active participants in workflows. New entrants are reshaping the SaaS landscape and turning multi-year contracts into strategic liabilities. Governance demands around data sovereignty, security, and responsible use of AI are expanding. And velocity mismatches between strategy, architecture, and governance remain the largest source of wasted IT investment today.
   

  

 We are seeking an
 **Enterprise Architect** 
 to design and govern the holistic blueprint that connects business strategy, applications, data, and technology across our clients’ organisations. Someone who can see the whole picture and make it coherent.
   

  

**Who is Umain?**
 We were founded with a
 **heritage in digital product development and engineering** 
 . This background gives us a unique perspective, ensuring that our strategic advice is always grounded in
 **technical feasibility and execution excellence** 
 . We are now expanding our focus to help clients at the C-level, linking core technology strategy directly to business outcomes.
   

  

 Umain is proudly part of
 **Eidra** 
 , a consultancy collective driven by the conviction that business transformation requires a combination of management consulting and best-in-breed expertise in creativity, engineering, and data. This partnership means that while you focus on strategic IT-Management and enterprise architecture, you have full access to orchestrate common capabilities from the best specialized companies within the collective.
   

  

 Our team consists of individuals with experience from the largest consulting firms, but with the flexibility, speed, and entrepreneurial drive that characterize a modern, flat organization. At Umain, we highly value
 **collaboration** 
 and
 **entrepreneurial spirit** 
 . Here, you are not only expected to lead demanding client assignments; you are also expected to actively contribute to our internal development, whether it involves shaping new service offerings in AI/Data or mentoring our junior talent. We reward those who take ownership and drive us forward.
   

  

**The Role: What will you do?**
 As An Enterprise Architect Within Eidra’s Tech Advisory & IT Management Practice, You Are The Structural Backbone Of Our Client Engagements. You Work Across Assessment, Design, And Transformation, Ensuring That Architectural Decisions Hold Up From Boardroom To Codebase. Your Assignments Will Involve
   

  

* Architecture Assessment & Scoring: Evaluating client technology estates for maturity, composability, technical debt, and swap-readiness. Producing actionable findings that connect directly to business outcomes and sourcing decisions.
* Enterprise Architecture Design: Designing composable, API-first architectures that replace rigid target-state blueprints with modular, swap-ready designs. Guiding major technology decisions on platforms, integration patterns, and cloud strategy.
* AI Readiness & Data Architecture: Assessing and designing the data and platform foundations needed for AI adoption. Designing model-agnostic AI layers that enable provider flexibility and reduce lock-in.
* Strategic Communication: Translating architecture decisions into business impact language for CIOs and boards. Connecting architecture findings to investment priorities, sourcing strategy, and risk posture.
* Internal Leadership: Actively contributing to Eidra’s business development, mentoring colleagues, and developing our architecture competency pool.


**Who are you?**
**We Are Looking For Someone Who**
* Has 7+ years of relevant experience in enterprise architecture, technology strategy, or IT transformation. Typical backgrounds include Enterprise Architect, Solution Architect, CTO, or Technology Advisor.
* Is driven by a deep passion for making complex technology landscapes coherent, composable, and aligned with business strategy.
* Has a documented ability to assess, design, and govern enterprise architecture across diverse industries and technology estates.
* Has a natural ability to structure and communicate complex architectural concepts to both engineering teams and C-suite stakeholders.


**Required Competencies**
* Enterprise Architecture Practice: Deep expertise in enterprise architecture frameworks adapted for modern, AI-era contexts. Experience maintaining architectural principles as standing policies and governing technology decisions across the enterprise.
* Platform & Integration Design: Hands-on experience with enterprise platforms and business-critical systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, modern data stacks), combined with strong expertise in integration architecture, API strategy, and cloud architecture.
* Composable Architecture: Ability to design modular, API-first architectures and assess technology components for swap-readiness, lock-in risk, and technical debt.
* Strategic AI: Solid understanding of how to design AI-ready platforms, including model-agnostic AI layers, data architecture for AI/ML workloads, and the implications of AI on enterprise architecture.
* Business-Technology Alignment: A proven ability to connect architecture decisions to business outcomes, investment cases, and sourcing strategy.
* Collaborative Mindset: A collaborative and open approach, with a genuine commitment to continuous development, team building, and long-term client relationships.
* Language: Fluency in English, both written and spoken, is essential.


 Do you want to help build the future digital backbone for our clients?
   

  

***Background check***
*Umain conducts a background check for final candidates in accordance with our internal policies. The check is carried out only with your consent and is ordered by our CISO through our approved screening partner. The scope of the check depends on the role level. This step is performed late in the recruitment process and is used solely to support a secure and fact‑based hiring decision.*
 Umain is a digital product consultancy. We shape, ship and scale digital products at speed. Founded in Stockholm in 2001, we are a growing team of 100+ experts in digital product strategy, design, and technology.
   

  

 We are a part of Eidra, a group of top-notch companies specializing in strategy, creativity, and engineering. As a collective we guide leaders in making meaningful and lasting change.
